perturbation.original_text: 7.8 The SENDER shall pay late interests on any unpaid amounts pursuant to the provisions set out by ECOPETROL in the Guidelines for Administration of Service Receivables ECP-UTE-G-008 or a document that modifies or supersedes it, which is an integral part of the Contract hereto as Annex 2.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.8 The SENDER shall pay late interests on any unpaid amounts pursuant to internal policies.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to 'Guidelines for Administration of Service Receivables ECP-UTE-G-008' and 'Annex 2', the modified text introduces uncertainty regarding the method of calculating late interests. Section 3.10 will now be referring to a document that no longer exists (the integral part being removed), creating a contradiction: 'Late interests shall be calculated on any overdue balances and in proportion to the time lapsed from the date in which the payment should have been made until that date in which it is actually made.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.10 Late Interests The payment of Receivables by clients after the due date generates a late interest as a sanction. Late interests are a applicable without exception to all kinds are Receivables handled by ECOPETROL. Late interests shall be calculated on any overdue balances and in proportion to the time lapsed from the date in which the payment should have been made until that date in which it is actually made. The record of these interests shall be under the responsibility of the Coordination of Receivables and collections; control and collection activities shall be the responsibility of the Business Unit providing the service, which may check these charges through consultations to the account statement of the client in the SAP integrated information system. Any payment made by a client in accordance with the provisions in the Colombian Civil Code in its article 1653, except as otherwise agreed, when principal and interests are owed, payment shall apply first to interests and then to principal. Late interests are not forgivable, the General Controller of the country has issued several opinions denying this prerogative based on the constitutional principle by which public servants can only do whatever the law and the Constitution allows. Currently there is no regulation that allows cancellation of interests by public entities such as ECOPETROL. ECOPETROL in each of its invoices shall indicate that the interest rate to be charged as late interest for the payment is the highest monthly rate allowed by the Superintendence of Finance or whoever replaces it (the Treasury Unit shall communicate the late interest rate to be applied for each period). For invoices or bills issued in US dollars an interest rate in dollars in equivalent to the Prime rate +2 percentage points shall be applied (Prime +2%). The resulting amounts shall be converted into pesos at the market representative exchange rate (TRM) valid on the date of payment. If the due date of the invoice falls on Saturday, Sunday or in a holiday, the payment may be made on the following Business day and said payment shall not generate any late interest. If a client pays after the first Business day, the calculation for late interest shall be made from the due date of the invoice The charging of interests shall be made through a bill addressed to the client, which shall contain a Receivables statement on which interests are calculated.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the specific reference to 'Guidelines for Administration of Service Receivables ECP-UTE-G-008' with 'internal policies.' This creates a contradiction because section 3.10 explicitly states how late interests 'shall be calculated,' implying a consistent methodology. By removing the specific guideline, the contract introduces uncertainty about the calculation method, thereby conflicting with the detailed calculation process described in section 3.10.

perturbation.original_text: 8.2 Otherwise, In order to guarantee compliance with all and each of the obligations of the SENDER under the Contract hereof, including but without being limited to the payment of the Fee, the SENDER is obliged to constitute in favor of ECOPETROL and to deliver within ten (10) business days after the subscription of the Contract hereof for the amount indicated in the Specific Conditions (hereinafter, the "Bond"): a) A performance policy for the payment of Services issued by an insurance policy legally established in the country, governed by the General Clauses of ECOPETROL indicated in Annex 3; or b) An irrevocable stand-by letter of credit at first requirement, issued by (i) a banking establishment authorized to operate in Colombia with AAA credit rating for its long-term debt in pesos, o (ii) a foreign financial entity with representation or a confirming and payment bank in Colombia, with risk credit of long term debt in dollars no less than the rating for the foreign sovereign debt of Colombia issued pursuant to the International Standby Practices (ISP98) of the International Chamber of Commerce, for which, it may be used the form contained in Annex 4 of the Contract hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.2 In order to guarantee compliance with the obligations of the SENDER under the Contract hereof, the SENDER is obliged to provide security.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the specific types of acceptable bonds, but not removing the 'Bond' mentioned later in the text in 8.3 and 8.5, ambiguity is introduced. This omission contradicts 8.5, 'The issuance and validity of the Bond shall be an indispensable condition for the provision of the Service,' because now the definition of the bond is not specified but the bond is mentioned creating an in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.5 The issuance and validity of the Bond shall be an indispensable condition for the provision of the Service. As a consequence, ECOPETROL may suspend the provision of the Service or terminate the Contract in advance, when the Bond is not in force, without this waiving the SENDER from its payment obligations and all other obligations derived from the Contract hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies acceptable forms of security ('Bond'). The changed text removes these specifics, only requiring 'security'. The contradicted text states that 'The issuance and validity of the Bond shall be an indispensable condition for the provision of the Service.' Because the Changed Text does not specify what a 'Bond' is anymore, it directly contradicts 8.5, as there is no definition of what needs to be issued. The definition was omitted in 8.2, so there's nothing to issue for 8.5, making them incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: 12.1 The minimum values of quality that the Crude delivered by the Senders must have to be accepted for Transportation in the Pipeline are: For specific systems the Transporter defines minimum parameters for quality which are listed in Annex 4 Minimum Quality Specifications by System. 12.2 Quality Certification.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.1 The minimum values of quality that the Crude delivered by the Senders must have to be accepted for Transportation in the Pipeline are: 12.2 Quality Certification.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to 'Annex 4 Minimum Quality Specifications by System', the contract no longer specifies the minimum quality parameters. Since in 12.2 we are referring to 'Quality Certification', creating an in-text contradiction, since without specifications, it is unclear what standard that certificate should meet.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.2 Quality Certification. 12.2.1 The Sender shall always provide the Transporter a certificate evidencing the characteristics and quality specifications of the Hydrocarbon, which shall be delivered to the Transporter. The certificate referred to in this numeral shall be issued by a company specialized in this matter and independent from the Sender. If the Sender fails to provide the corresponding certificate, the Transporter shall not have the obligation to accept or transport the Hydrocarbon through the Pipeline. 12.2.2 The minimum characteristics of the Hydrocarbon which must be included in the certificate are: Viscosity cST and SSU at 86°F, 100°F and 140°F, gravity API at 60°F, sulfur content, salt content, BSW, Acidity and Point of fluidity.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly refers to 'Annex 4 Minimum Quality Specifications by System,' defining the minimum quality parameters. The changed text removes this reference, leaving the contract without specified quality parameters. The contradicted text, 12.2, mandates 'Quality Certification' and details characteristics that 'must be included in the certificate.' Without the reference to Annex 4, it is unclear what standards the certification should adhere to, creating a clear contradiction and making the certification requirement unenforceable. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition because essential language was removed in one section, creating a contradiction with obligations in another section.
